mapping.a_element: large-market limit (number of agents grows to infinity)
mapping.b_element: mean-field limit (number of Kac particles N grows to infinity)

mapping.a_element: canonical random model of agents' preferences and priorities
mapping.b_element: random particle system with prescribed random initial datum / empirical measure

mapping.a_element: asymptotic convergence of TTC's justified-envy incidence to that of RSD
mapping.b_element: quantitative convergence of the empirical measure to the Boltzmann weak solution in Wasserstein-2 distance

brief_justification: Both are large-system limit theorems, but the driving machinery differs: A analyzes a combinatorial assignment mechanism (top-trading-cycles cycle-clearing) and shows two matching mechanisms become statistically equivalent in the large market, whereas B is a mean-field propagation-of-chaos argument deriving a nonlinear kinetic PDE (the Boltzmann equation) from stochastic collision dynamics with an explicit Wasserstein rate. The only overlap is the generic 'take size to infinity and prove convergence,' not a shared governing process or role-for-role mechanism.
